title: Possible 20th Anniversary Edition Blu Ray or Digital
display_title: Possible 20th Anniversary Edition Blu Ray or Digital
wikitext:
[]: Possible 20th Anniversary Edition: Blu-Ray (or Digital)             Quote Post by M0arDakka » Sat Oct 20, 2012 2:08 am Following a look-over of my box set and copy of EoE, and my inquiry into the Japanese version of the most recent release, I've been considering the likelihood of a 2015 release of Evangelion by Section23, since as far as I can tell they have been faring well in recent times. So, what do you think about a Blu-Ray or even digital download version of the original series?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Oct 20, 2012 2:12 am Blu Reis* Anyway, I think a while back someone said it was more complicated to release an HD update of Eva than other shows for some reason? I cant' recall but I'm sure someone will explain if I'm right or chastise me for being wrong. I'd buy them though.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Oct 20, 2012 2:19 am CJD wrote: Blu Reis*

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Oct 20, 2012 11:46 am EoE on Blu-ray: YES. PLEASE MAKE IT HAPPEN. A Re-remaster of the film would be good. Death (true) ^2 on Blu: PASS. As for NGE on Blu: well, the thing is: that's what the Rebuilds are for: taking advantage of the HD animation,and to this day, they're still demo-worthy discs. It'd be hard to sell the original since the highlight for EVA on Blu is, again, the rebuild films (though, story-wise, The original show is better). Also, even if it was finalized on 16mm prints, it doesn't mean that they won't look better than the DVDs. Basically, it can be done, but it all depends on the demand. ... that, or maybe they'll release the original in HD after dealing with all the Rebuild films. That would make sense. For Digital Download: ... isn't the dub shown on the PS3 and X-Box 360? Sure there's no Japanese version included and it's not in HD, but the show should be there. It's been awhile since I had seen that at the PSN store. But Seriously, DVD/Blu-ray GREATER THAN digital downloads. End of story.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Oct 20, 2012 12:22 pm tomrule123 wrote: EoE on Blu-ray: YES. PLEASE MAKE IT HAPPEN. A Re-remaster of the film would be good. Death (true) ^2 on Blu: PASS.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Oct 20, 2012 1:16 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Lol, if they keep the format of the Renewal (single discs) then you wouldn't have much of a choice, they're sold together (Dynit did the same for the Italian release too).

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sat Oct 20, 2012 2:58 pm Yes. EoE needs to be given a proper, film-source telecined brand-new remaster to 1080p resolution. I would say the same applies to Death(true)^2 too. I would buy. I only hope there still exists a film copies of sufficient quality of those two films when they'll get around to it, if ever. Also, I also hope whoever ends up doing this, will have to forward-thinking ability (and budget) to go straight for the 4K resolution while they're at it. About the series... meh. Only way it will have better picture quality would be a case when they rescan the whole thing from source instead of simple upscale of DVD source. And that... would be quite lot of money down the drain for a very little visible gain. I hope they won't waste resources on that. Really. I mean, I really don't think there's much of anything in the visuals of NGE that would get any significant improvement from a retransfer to HD as it's 16mm source of 90s TV animation that isn't already done in renewal/platinum. I know this might sound blasphemous but ... the visual quality and detailedness of TV evangelion just isn't *that* good to begin with . Keywords: "1995". "TV animation". "16mm".

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Oct 20, 2012 3:35 pm Luckily Evangelion isn't that long, unlike LoGH just to mention a "random" title which has been upscaled for its BD release, probably I'm starting to sound like a broken record but Nadia has almost twice the episodes... sure, Nadia's old transfer looked worse than shit but this is Evangelion, I'm sure that they would recover the investment of rescanning the films in little time, it's more of a matter of "when" rather than "if", the 20th anniversary could be an excellent chance like M0arDakka suggested. Also, Renewal was released almost a decade ago, by now almost everyone interested in it already bought it, instead a new BD Box would find a lot of new potential buyers.

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Mon Oct 22, 2012 4:17 pm If nothing else, a remastered EoE on Blu Rei would be great. Death (True)^2 would be nice, but it's okay if not. And I say either redub it or release it as Subs Only. As for the series. It'd be nice to see a release of it, maybe with some great special features, commentary, or vignettes. I'm a sucker for stuff like that. It's needless to say that something will come around for the 20th Anniversary. Just a matter of what.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Oct 22, 2012 4:23 pm Getting all the Eva conten on Blu-Rei would be awesome. I would be nice to replace the old Japanese copy of EoE.

[]: Re: Possible 20th Anniversary Edition: Blu-Ray (or Digital)             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 22, 2012 6:27 pm M0arDakka wrote: I've been considering the likelihood of a 2015 release of Evangelion by Section23, since as far as I can tell they have been faring well in recent times.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 22, 2012 8:24 pm An HD progressive scan of 16mm film has been done before with other GAINAX shows and OVAs, like Gunbuster , so I really don't see that in particular stopping them from a Blu-ray release. But didn't GAINAX loose the films reels to a couple of their NGE episodes? I thought I heard about that somewhere, and it would make sense that entire episodes missing on 16mm film would be the only thing stopping GAINAX from a Blu-ray release of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 22, 2012 10:05 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: But didn't GAINAX loose the films reels to a couple of their NGE episodes? I thought I heard about that somewhere, and it would make sense that entire episodes missing on 16mm film would be the only thing stopping GAINAX from a Blu-ray release of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Wed Oct 24, 2012 1:18 am If they put it on Blu-Rei, they'd better focus on color correction, rather than getting rid of the grain which gets rid of detail. It did wonders for Utena, it did wonders for Ghibli films, it could do wonders for Evangelion. One thing I can't stand about the remaster is how muddled everything looks. Sure, you don't have any more grain, but you lose detail and the end product looks all smudged-up.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Oct 24, 2012 2:51 am ^ About the grain, I'm not sure why you fear that they would do it in the first place, Dynit likes to mess up with my grain, and amazingly enough a lot of persons actually like it better this way, Gainax doesn't seem to take any pleasure in doing it. What do you mean with colour correction instead? Were the colours of the Renewal somehow off? Utena's colours look good if you compare them with the old version but the same is true for Evangelion as well, as far as I can see and remember.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Wed Oct 24, 2012 12:50 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ About the grain, I'm not sure why you fear that they would do it in the first place, Dynit likes to mess up with my grain, and amazingly enough a lot of persons actually like it better this way, Gainax doesn't seem to take any pleasure in doing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 24, 2012 5:33 pm How about we just "Rebuild" the entire series? Same exact shots, same animation (mostly), but we make everything prettier. Misato can finally get a consistent design!

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 24, 2012 5:36 pm Warren Peace wrote: How about we just "Rebuild" the entire series? Same exact shots, same animation (mostly), but we make everything prettier. Misato can finally get a consistent design!

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 24, 2012 5:37 pm Renewal/Platinum's sound mix has been pretty much Rebuilt anyway, so why not go all the way?

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Wed Oct 24, 2012 6:24 pm Dream wrote: Completely in favor of this.
